description: The new matcher protects /api/health, but on origin/main /api/health was excluded (matcher was `/api/((?!health|webhook).*)`). The current README at lines 73-78 still documents `GET /api/health` as a 'Health check (no auth)' endpoint. Calls without a session cookie or bearer token now return 401 instead of `{status:'ok'}`, which silently breaks any external uptime probe pointed at the deployed Vercel URL.
suggestion: Add `api/health` to the matcher's negative lookahead alongside `api/login` / `api/webhook` so the endpoint stays public, OR update the README to reflect the new behavior. Restoring the exclusion is the lower-friction fix and matches existing documentation.

description: `HabitCompletion.section: string` and `sectionOrder: number` are typed as non-null, but `app/api/habits/completions/route.ts:27-28` forwards `h.sectionName` / `h.sectionOrder` from `queryHabitCompletions`, where both are `string | null` (LEFT JOIN against `sections`). A habit assigned to no Todoist section returns `null` and (a) is keyed as `null` in `groupBySection`'s Map, (b) sorts with `null - null = NaN` in `groups.sort`, and (c) renders an empty `<h2>`. This isn't a crash but the section-grouping invariant the plan calls out (use `sectionOrder`) silently breaks.
suggestion: Either tighten the API to provide defaults (e.g. `section: h.sectionName ?? 'Uncategorized'`, `sectionOrder: h.sectionOrder ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`) or widen `HabitCompletion` to `section: string | null` / `sectionOrder: number | null` and make `groupBySection` coalesce explicitly. The first option preserves the plan's grouping intent more cleanly.

description: `fetchHabitCompletions` falls back to `http://localhost:3000` when `INTERNAL_BASE_URL` is unset. The existing README documents step 6 'Deploy to Vercel', and `.env.example` does not list `INTERNAL_BASE_URL`. After deploy, the server component's self-fetch will try localhost, fail, and the dashboard will not render. Local dev is fine; Vercel is not.
suggestion: Either (a) call the database directly from the server component instead of round-tripping through the HTTP API (preferred — avoids the cookie-forwarding dance entirely), or (b) widen the fallback chain to include `process.env.VERCEL_URL ? \`https://${process.env.VERCEL_URL}\` : 'http://localhost:3000'` and add `INTERNAL_BASE_URL` to `.env.example` with a comment about Vercel.

description: The day-grid container claims `role="grid"` but its DayCell descendants have no `role="row"` or `role="gridcell"`. Asserting `role="grid"` on an element whose children don't follow the WAI-ARIA grid pattern is worse than leaving the implicit semantics in place — assistive tech may announce the container as a grid and then fail to navigate it correctly.
suggestion: Drop `role="grid"` and `aria-label` from the cell-container div (the visible header text already labels the section), or wrap each week in a `role="row"` and give DayCell `role="gridcell"`. Removing the role is the smaller change and a net improvement.

description: Only the `skipped` branch carries a screen-reader status (`{day} (skipped)`). Completed and pending cells render only the bare day number, so an AT user cannot distinguish a completed day from a pending one — both are read as just '15'.
suggestion: Add an sr-only suffix on the completed branch (` (completed)`) or move status into an `aria-label` on the outer cell. Pending can stay bare since it is the default state.

description: `docs/implementation/smoke-test/result.json` and `stdout.json` arrived in this PR via the housekeeping commit `089b49c`. They aren't related to the habits-calendar feature and aren't referenced anywhere. Context.md flags this travels with the PR. It clutters the diff and `docs/implementation/` is meant for orchestration artifacts of real plans.
suggestion: Delete `docs/implementation/smoke-test/` from the branch. Either drop those two files via a follow-up commit before merge, or amend the housekeeping commit out (it's the very first commit on the branch).

description: The logout endpoint exists but no UI surface invokes it — there is no logout button or link anywhere in the dashboard. The plan's Step 2 only required the endpoint, so this is plan-conforming, but practically the user has no way to clear their session short of devtools.
suggestion: Add a small 'Sign out' link in the page footer or near the MonthHeader that posts to `/api/logout` and redirects to `/login`. Defer to v2 if scope creep is a concern, but worth a single-line entry in the v2 follow-up list.

description: `key={date.toISOString()}` works but is heavier than necessary — the date is already known to be unique within a month grid, and a simpler `formatYmd(date)` key would match the YMD identity used everywhere else in the file. Trivial perf/style nit.
suggestion: Switch to `key={formatYmd(date)}` for consistency with the rest of the date handling. Optional.
